message.content:
## 目标
在 /Users/admin/it-asset 项目中创建前端通用层：shadcn/ui 组件、布局、业务组件、认证上下文、API 客户端。

## 项目状态
- Next.js 16 App Router + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS v4
- shadcn/ui 已初始化，button.tsx 已安装
- lucide-react 已安装
- `lib/utils.ts` 已有 (shadcn 工具函数)
- API 已有: auth (login/logout/session), tickets (全流程), assets, AI (recommend/triage/chat)
- AUTH_SECRET: `"it-asset-dev-secret-change-in-production"`

## 任务 A: 安装 shadcn/ui 组件
```bash
npx shadcn add badge card dialog input textarea skeleton separator sheet dropdown-menu avatar scroll-area -y
```

## 任务 B: 布局组件

### 1. `components/layout/app-layout.tsx`
```tsx
"use client";
// h-screen flex, 左侧 Sidebar(desktop: md:flex hidden, mobile: Sheet), 右侧 Header+main
// Header 有 menu 按钮控制 mobile sidebar
```

### 2. `components/layout/sidebar.tsx`
角色路由菜单（22项），lucide-react 图标：
```typescript
type NavItem = { label: string; href: string; icon: React.ElementType; roles: UserRole[]; };

const navItems: NavItem[] = [
  { label: "首页", href: "/home", icon: LayoutDashboard, roles: ["EMPLOYEE","MANAGER","IT_ADMIN","EXECUTIVE"] },
  { label: "我的资产", href: "/my-assets", icon: Laptop, roles: ["EMPLOYEE","MANAGER"] },
  { label: "申领设备", href: "/apply", icon: ClipboardPlus, roles: ["EMPLOYEE","MANAGER"] },
  { label: "我的工单", href: "/tickets", icon: Ticket, roles: ["EMPLOYEE","MANAGER"] },
  { label: "AI 助手", href: "/chat", icon: Bot, roles: ["EMPLOYEE","MANAGER","IT_ADMIN","EXECUTIVE"] },
  { label: "通知中心", href: "/notifications", icon: Bell, roles: ["EMPLOYEE","MANAGER","IT_ADMIN","EXECUTIVE"] },
  { label: "个人设置", href: "/profile", icon: Settings, roles: ["EMPLOYEE","MANAGER","IT_ADMIN","EXECUTIVE"] },
  { label: "审批工作台", href: "/approvals", icon: CheckSquare, roles: ["MANAGER"] },
  { label: "部门看板", href: "/dept-board", icon: BarChart3, roles: ["MANAGER"] },
  { label: "IT 工作台", href: "/it/home", icon: Wrench, roles: ["IT_ADMIN"] },
  { label: "工单工作台", href: "/it/tickets", icon: Ticket, roles: ["IT_ADMIN"] },
  { label: "资产管理", href: "/it/assets", icon: Package, roles: ["IT_ADMIN"] },
  { label: "型号管理", href: "/it/models", icon: Archive, roles: ["IT_ADMIN"] },
  { label: "入库操作", href: "/it/inbound", icon: Boxes, roles: ["IT_ADMIN"] },
  { label: "盘点", href: "/it/inventory", icon: ClipboardList, roles: ["IT_ADMIN"] },
  { label: "用户管理", href: "/it/users", icon: Users, roles: ["IT_ADMIN"] },
  { label: "审计日志", href: "/it/audit", icon: FileText, roles: ["IT_ADMIN"] },
  { label: "资产大屏", href: "/exec/dashboard", icon: PieChart, roles: ["EXECUTIVE"] },
  { label: "AI 洞察", href: "/exec/insights", icon: Lightbulb, roles: ["EXECUTIVE"] },
];
// filterNavItems(items, role) 过滤
// usePathname() 判断 active
// 底部 RoleSwitcher: 4 角色调试切换 (useAuth().setRole)
```
品牌区：显示"资产管理系统"+"{部门} · {用户名}"
Icon: 从 lucide-react 导入所有图标

### 3. `components/layout/header.tsx`
```tsx
"use client";
// 面包屑（从 pathname 推导）, 右侧 NotificationBell + Avatar
// Menu 按钮 md:hidden 控制移动端 sidebar
// onMenuClick prop
```

## 任务 C: 业务组件

### 4. `components/asset-status-badge.tsx`
根据 AssetInstanceStatus 显示不同颜色的 Badge:
- IN_STOCK=gray, AVAILABLE=green, ASSIGNED=blue, IN_USE=teal, RETURNING=yellow, REPAIRING=orange, TRANSFERRING=purple, LOST=red, SCRAPPED=gray
- 中文映射

### 5. `components/empty-state.tsx`
图标 + title + description + 可选 action 按钮（支持 href 或 onClick）

### 6. `components/notification-bell.tsx`
铃铛图标 + 未读数 Badge，从 useAuth 取 unreadNotifications

### 7. `components/role-guard.tsx`
```tsx
interface RoleGuardProps { roles: UserRole[]; children: React.ReactNode; fallback?: React.ReactNode; }
// useAuth().user.role 不在 roles 中时返回 fallback (默认 null)
```

### 8. `components/ticket-timeline.tsx`
工单时间轴组件，接收 events: {label, time, description?}[]
垂直时间轴样式，圆形指示器+连接线

## 任务 D: 认证上下文 + API 客户端

### 9. `lib/auth-context.tsx`
```tsx
"use client";
export type UserRole = "EMPLOYEE" | "MANAGER" | "IT_ADMIN" | "EXECUTIVE";
export interface User { id: string; name: string; email: string; role: UserRole; avatar?: string; department: string; departmentId: string; }

interface AuthContextType {
  user: User; token: string | null; loading: boolean;
  login(email, password): Promise<void>;  // POST /api/auth/login, setToken, setUser
  logout(): void;                         // clearToken, setUser(defaultUser)
  setRole(role): void;
  unreadNotifications: number;
  setUnreadNotifications(n: number): void;
}

// AuthProvider: useEffect 启动时从 localStorage 读 token，GET /api/auth/session 恢复用户
// 未登录时用 defaultUser (EMPLOYEE 张明)
// login 调用 POST /api/auth/login → 存 token 到 localStorage + state
```

### 10. `lib/api-client.ts`
```tsx
const TOKEN_KEY = "auth-token";
function getToken(): string | null  // localStorage.getItem
export function setToken(token: string)  // localStorage.setItem
export function clearToken()  // localStorage.removeItem

export async function apiFetch<T = unknown>(url: string, options: RequestInit = {}): Promise<T> {
  // 自动注入 Authorization: Bearer {token} + Content-Type: application/json
  // 401 → clearToken
  // 非 ok → throw ApiError(status, message)
}
export class ApiError extends Error { status: number; }
```

## 任务 E: 更新 app/layout.tsx
用 AppLayout 包裹 children，AuthProvider 包裹整个应用。

## 验收
- 所有文件存在，TypeScript 零错误
- 布局三件套 (app-layout, sidebar, header) 完整
- 5 个业务组件完整
- auth-context + api-client 就绪
- 角色路由 22 项正确过滤

1 --- 2 title: layout.js 3 description: API reference for the layout.js file. 4 --- 5 6 The `layout` file is used to define a layout in your Next.js application. 7 8 ```tsx filename="app/dashboard/layout.tsx" switcher 9 export default function DashboardLayout({ 10 children, 11 }: { 12 children: React.ReactNode 13 }) { 14 return
{children}
15 } 16 ``` 17 18 ```jsx filename="app/dashboard/layout.js" switcher 19 export default function DashboardLayout({ children }) { 20 return
{children}
21 } 22 ``` 23 24 In the [component hierarchy](/docs/app/getting-started/project-structure#component-hierarchy), `layout.js` is the outermost component in a route segment. It wraps `template.js`, `error.js`, `loading.js`, `not-found.js`, and `page.js`. 25 26 A **root layout** is the top-most layout in the root `app` directory. It is used to define the `` and `` tags and other globally shared UI. 27 28 ```tsx filename="app/layout.tsx" switcher 29 export default function RootLayout({ 30 children, 31 }: { 32 children: React.ReactNode 33 }) { 34 return ( 35 36 {children} 37 38 ) 39 } 40 ``` 41 42 ```jsx filename="app/layout.js" switcher 43 export default function RootLayout({ children }) { 44 return ( 45 46 {children} 47 48 ) 49 } 50 ``` 51 52 ## Reference 53 54 ### Props 55 56 #### `children` (required) 57 58 Layout components should accept and use a `children` prop. During rendering, `children` will be populated with the route segments the layout is wrapping. These will primarily be the component of a child [Layout](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/page) (if it exists) or [Page](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/page), but could also be other special files like [Loading](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/loading) or [Error](/docs/app/getting-started/error-handling) when applicable. 59 60 #### `params` (optional) 61 62 A promise that resolves to an object containing the [dynamic route parameters](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/dynamic-routes) object from the root segment down to that layout. 63 64 ```tsx filename="app/dashboard/[team]/layout.tsx" switcher 65 export default async function Layout({ 66 children, 67 params, 68 }: { 69 children: React.ReactNode 70 params: Promise<{ team: string }> 71 }) { 72 const { team } = await params 73 } 74 ``` 75 76 ```jsx filename="app/dashboard/[team]/layout.js" switcher 77 export default async function Layout({ children, params }) { 78 const { team } = await params 79 } 80 ``` 81 82 | Example Route | URL | `params` | 83 | --------------------------------- | -------------- | ---------------------------------- | 84 | `app/dashboard/[team]/layout.js` | `/dashboard/1` | `Promise<{ team: '1' }>` | 85 | `app/shop/[tag]/[item]/layout.js` | `/shop/1/2` | `Promise<{ tag: '1', item: '2' }>` | 86 | `app/blog/[...slug]/layout.js` | `/blog/1/2` | `Promise<{ slug: ['1', '2'] }>` | 87 88 - Since the `params` prop is a promise. You must use `async/await` or React's [`use`](https://react.dev/reference/react/use) function to access the values. 89 - In version 14 and earlier, `params` was a synchronous prop. To help with backwards compatibility, you can still access it synchronously in Next.js 15, but this behavior will be deprecated in the future. 90 91 ### Layout Props Helper 92 93 You can type layouts with `LayoutProps` to get a strongly typed `params` and named slots inferred from your directory structure. `LayoutProps` is a globally available helper. 94 95 ```tsx filename="app/dashboard/layout.tsx" 96 export default function Layout(props: LayoutProps<'/dashboard'>) { 97 return ( 98
99 {props.children} 100 {/* If you have app/dashboard/@analytics, it appears as a typed slot: */} 101 {/* {props.analytics} */} 102
103 ) 104 } 105 ``` 106 107 > **Good to know**: 108 > 109 > - Types are generated during `next dev`, `next build` or `next typegen`. 110 > - After type generation, the `LayoutProps` helper is globally available. It doesn't need to be imported. 111 112 ### Root Layout 113 114 The `app` directory **must** include a **root layout**, which is the top-most layout in the root `app` directory. Typically, the root layout is `app/layout.js`. 115 116 ```tsx filename="app/layout.tsx" switcher 117 export default function RootLayout({ 118 children, 119 }: { 120 children: React.ReactNode 121 }) { 122 return ( 123 124 {children} 125 126 ) 127 } 128 ``` 129 130 ```jsx filename="app/layout.js" switcher 131 export default function RootLayout({ children }) { 132 return ( 133 134 {children} 135 136 ) 137 } 138 ``` 139 140 - The root layout **must** define `` and `` tags. 141 - You should **not** manually add `` tags such as `` and `<meta>` to root layouts. Instead, you should use the [Metadata API](/docs/app/getting-started/metadata-and-og-images) which automatically handles advanced requirements such as streaming and de-duplicating `<head>` elements. 142 - You can create **multiple root layouts**. Any layout without a `layout.js` above it is a root layout. Two common approaches: 143 - Using [route groups](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/route-groups) like `app/(shop)/layout.js` and `app/(marketing)/layout.js` 144 - Omitting `app/layout.js` so layouts in subdirectories like `app/dashboard/layout.js` and `app/blog/layout.js` each become root layouts for their respective directories. 145 - Navigating **across multiple root layouts** will cause a **full page load** (as opposed to a client-side navigation). 146 - The root layout can be under a **dynamic segment**, for example when implementing [internationalization](/docs/app/guides/internationalization) with `app/[lang]/layout.js`. 147 148 ## Caveats 149 150 ### Request Object 151 152 Layouts are cached in the client during navigation to avoid unnecessary server requests. 153 154 [Layouts](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/layout) do not rerender. They can be cached and reused to avoid unnecessary computation when navigating between pages. By restricting layouts from accessing the raw request, Next.js can prevent the execution of potentially slow or expensive user code within the layout, which could negatively impact performance. 155 156 To access the request object, you can use [`headers`](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/headers) and [`cookies`](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/cookies) APIs in [Server Components](/docs/app/getting-started/server-and-client-components) and Functions. 157 158 ```tsx filename="app/shop/layout.tsx" switcher 159 import { cookies } from 'next/headers' 160 161 export default async function Layout({ children }) { 162 const cookieStore = await cookies() 163 const theme = cookieStore.get('theme') 164 return '...' 165 } 166 ``` 167 168 ```jsx filename="app/shop/layout.js" switcher 169 import { cookies } from 'next/headers' 170 171 export default async function Layout({ children }) { 172 const cookieStore = await cookies() 173 const theme = cookieStore.get('theme') 174 return '...' 175 } 176 ``` 177 178 ### Query params 179 180 Layouts do not rerender on navigation, so they cannot access search params which would otherwise become stale. 181 182 To access updated query parameters, you can use the Page [`searchParams`](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/page#searchparams-optional) prop, or read them inside a Client Component using the [`useSearchParams`](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/use-search-params) hook. Since Client Components re-render on navigation, they have access to the latest query parameters. 183 184 ```tsx filename="app/ui/search.tsx" switcher 185 'use client' 186 187 import { useSearchParams } from 'next/navigation' 188 189 export default function Search() { 190 const searchParams = useSearchParams() 191 192 const search = searchParams.get('search') 193 194 return '...' 195 } 196 ``` 197 198 ```jsx filename="app/ui/search.js" switcher 199 'use client' 200 201 import { useSearchParams } from 'next/navigation' 202 203 export default function Search() { 204 const searchParams = useSearchParams() 205 206 const search = searchParams.get('search') 207 208 return '...' 209 } 210 ``` 211 212 ```tsx filename="app/shop/layout.tsx" switcher 213 import Search from '@/app/ui/search' 214 215 export default function Layout({ children }) { 216 return ( 217 <> 218 <Search /> 219 {children} 220 </> 221 ) 222 } 223 ``` 224 225 ```jsx filename="app/shop/layout.js" switcher 226 import Search from '@/app/ui/search' 227 228 export default function Layout({ children }) { 229 return ( 230 <> 231 <Search /> 232 {children} 233 </> 234 ) 235 } 236 ``` 237 238 ### Pathname 239 240 Layouts do not re-render on navigation, so they do not access pathname which would otherwise become stale. 241 242 To access the current pathname, you can read it inside a Client Component using the [`usePathname`](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/use-pathname) hook. Since Client Components re-render during navigation, they have access to the latest pathname. 243 244 ```tsx filename="app/ui/breadcrumbs.tsx" switcher 245 'use client' 246 247 import { usePathname } from 'next/navigation' 248 249 // Simplified breadcrumbs logic 250 export default function Breadcrumbs() { 251 const pathname = usePathname() 252 const segments = pathname.split('/') 253 254 return ( 255 <nav> 256 {segments.map((segment, index) => ( 257 <span key={index}> 258 {' > '} 259 {segment} 260 </span> 261 ))} 262 </nav> 263 ) 264 } 265 ``` 266 267 ```jsx filename="app/ui/breadcrumbs.js" switcher 268 'use client' 269 270 import { usePathname } from 'next/navigation' 271 272 // Simplified breadcrumbs logic 273 export default function Breadcrumbs() { 274 const pathname = usePathname() 275 const segments = pathname.split('/') 276 277 return ( 278 <nav> 279 {segments.map((segment, index) => ( 280 <span key={index}> 281 {' > '} 282 {segment} 283 </span> 284 ))} 285 </nav> 286 ) 287 } 288 ``` 289 290 ```tsx filename="app/docs/layout.tsx" switcher 291 import { Breadcrumbs } from '@/app/ui/Breadcrumbs' 292 293 export default function Layout({ children }) { 294 return ( 295 <> 296 <Breadcrumbs /> 297 <main>{children}</main> 298 </> 299 ) 300 } 301 ``` 302 303 ```jsx filename="app/docs/layout.js" switcher 304 import { Breadcrumbs } from '@/app/ui/Breadcrumbs' 305 306 export default function Layout({ children }) { 307 return ( 308 <> 309 <Breadcrumbs /> 310 <main>{children}</main> 311 </> 312 ) 313 } 314 ``` 315 316 ### Interaction with `loading.js` 317 318 Because `loading.js` sits below `layout.js` in the [component hierarchy](/docs/app/getting-started/project-structure#component-hierarchy), it cannot show a fallback for uncached or runtime data access in the layout itself, such as calling [`cookies()`](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/cookies), [`headers()`](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/headers), or making uncached fetches. 319 320 The behavior depends on whether [Cache Components](/docs/app/getting-started/caching) is enabled: 321 322 - **Without Cache Components:** The navigation will block until the layout finishes rendering, and the `loading.js` fallback will not be shown. 323 - **With Cache Components:** `loading.js` is treated as a regular `<Suspense>` boundary rather than a special prefetch marker. Uncached or runtime data access in the layout must be explicitly wrapped in its own `<Suspense>` boundary, otherwise Next.js guides you with a build-time error. The static shell streams immediately, and the uncached content swaps in as it resolves. 324 325 In both cases, to ensure instant navigation, either: 326 327 - Wrap runtime data access in your layout in its own `<Suspense>` boundary with a fallback. 328 - Move uncached data fetching from `layout.js` into `page.js` where `loading.js` can show a fallback. 329 330 ```tsx filename="app/dashboard/layout.tsx" switcher highlight={8-10} 331 import { Suspense } from 'react' 332 import { NavSkeleton } from './nav-skeleton' 333 import { DashboardNav } from './dashboard-nav' 334 335 export default function Layout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) { 336 return ( 337 <> 338 <Suspense fallback={<NavSkeleton />}> 339 <DashboardNav /> 340 </Suspense> 341 <main>{children}</main> 342 </> 343 ) 344 } 345 ``` 346 347 ```jsx filename="app/dashboard/layout.js" switcher highlight={8-10} 348 import { Suspense } from 'react' 349 import { NavSkeleton } from './nav-skeleton' 350 import { DashboardNav } from './dashboard-nav' 351 352 export default function Layout({ children }) { 353 return ( 354 <> 355 <Suspense fallback={<NavSkeleton />}> 356 <DashboardNav /> 357 </Suspense> 358 <main>{children}</main> 359 </> 360 ) 361 } 362 ``` 363 364 ### Fetching Data 365 366 Layouts cannot pass data to their `children`. However, you can fetch the same data in a route more than once, and use React [`cache`](https://react.dev/reference/react/cache) to dedupe the requests without affecting performance. 367 368 Alternatively, when using [`fetch`](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/fetch)in Next.js, requests are automatically deduped. 369 370 ```tsx filename="app/lib/data.ts" switcher 371 export async function getUser(id: string) { 372 const res = await fetch(`https://.../users/${id}`) 373 return res.json() 374 } 375 ``` 376 377 ```tsx filename="app/dashboard/layout.tsx" switcher 378 import { getUser } from '@/app/lib/data' 379 import { UserName } from '@/app/ui/user-name' 380 381 export default async function Layout({ children }) { 382 const user = await getUser('1') 383 384 return ( 385 <> 386 <nav> 387 {/* ... */} 388 <UserName user={user.name} /> 389 </nav> 390 {children} 391 </> 392 ) 393 } 394 ``` 395 396 ```jsx filename="app/dashboard/layout.js" switcher 397 import { getUser } from 433 434 export default async function Page() { 435 const user = await getUser('1') 436 437 return ( 438 <div> 439 <h1>Welcome {user.name}</h1> 440 </div> 441 ) 442 } 443 ``` 444 445 ### Accessing child segments 446 447 Layouts do not have access to the route segments below itself. To access all route segments, you can use [`useSelectedLayoutSegment`](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/use-selected-layout-segment) or [`useSelectedLayoutSegments`](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/use-selected-layout-segments) in a Client Component. 448 449 ```tsx filename="app/ui/nav-link.tsx" switcher 450 'use client' 451 452 import Link from 'next/link' 453 import { useSelectedLayoutSegment } from 'next/navigation' 454 455 export default function NavLink({ 456 slug, 457 children, 458 }: { 459 slug: string 460 children: React.ReactNode 461 }) { 462 const segment = useSelectedLayoutSegment() 463 const isActive = slug === segment 464 465 return ( 466 <Link 467 href={`/blog/${slug}`} 468 // Change style depending on whether the link is active 469 style={{ fontWeight: isActive ? 'bold' : 'normal' }} 470 > 471 {children} 472 </Link> 473 ) 474 } 475 ``` 476 477 ```jsx filename="app/ui/nav-link.js" switcher 478 'use client' 479 480 import Link from 'next/link' 481 import { useSelectedLayoutSegment } from 'next/navigation' 482 483 export default function NavLinks({ slug, children }) { 484 const segment = useSelectedLayoutSegment() 485 const isActive = slug === segment 486 487 return ( 488 <Link 489 href={`/blog/${slug}`} 490 style={{ fontWeight: isActive ? 'bold' : 'normal' }} 491 > 492 {children} 493 </Link> 494 ) 495 } 496 ``` 497 498 ```tsx filename="app/blog/layout.tsx" switcher 499 import { NavLink } from './nav-link' 500 import getPosts from './get-posts' 501 502 export default async function Layout({ 503 children, 504 }: { 505 children: React.ReactNode 506 }) { 507 const featuredPosts = await getPosts() 508 return ( 509 <div> 510 {featuredPosts.map((post) => ( 511 <div key={post.id}> 512 <NavLink slug={post.slug}>{post.title}</NavLink> 513 </div> 514 ))} 515 <div>{children}</div> 516 </div> 517 ) 518 } 519 ``` 520 521 ```jsx filename="app/blog/layout.js" switcher 522 import { NavLink } from './nav-link' 523 import getPosts from './get-posts' 524 525 export default async function Layout({ children }) { 526 const featuredPosts = await getPosts() 527 return ( 528 <div> 529 {featuredPosts.map((post) => ( 530 <div key={post.id}> 531 <NavLink slug={post.slug}>{post.title}</NavLink> 532 </div> 533 ))} 534 <div>{children}</div> 535 </div> 536 ) 537 } 538 ``` 539 540 ## Examples 541 542 ### Metadata 543 544 You can modify the `<head>` HTML elements such as `title` and `meta` using the [`metadata` object](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/generate-metadata#the-metadata-object) or [`generateMetadata` function](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/generate-metadata#generatemetadata-function). 545 546 ```tsx filename="app/layout.tsx" switcher 547 import type { Metadata } from 'next' 548 549 export const metadata: Metadata = { 550 title: 'Next.js', 551 } 552 553 export default function Layout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) { 554 return '...' 555 } 556 ``` 557 558 ```jsx filename="app/layout.js" switcher 559 export const metadata = { 560 title: 'Next.js', 561 } 562 563 export default function Layout({ children }) { 564 return '...' 565 } 566 ``` 567 568 > **Good to know**: You should **not** manually add `<head>` tags such as `<title>` and `<meta>` to root layouts. Instead, use the [Metadata APIs](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/generate-metadata) which automatically handles advanced requirements such as streaming and de-duplicating `<head>` elements. 569 570 ### Active Nav Links 571 572 You can use the [`usePathname`](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/use-pathname) hook to determine if a nav link is active. 573 574 Since `usePathname` is a client hook, you need to extract the nav links into a Client Component, which can be imported into your layout: 575 576 ```tsx filename="app/ui/nav-links.tsx" switcher 577 'use client' 578 579 import { usePathname } from 'next/navigation' 580 import Link from 'next/link' 581 582 export function NavLinks() { 583 const pathname = usePathname() 584 585 return ( 586 <nav> 587 <Link className={`link ${pathname === '/' ? 'active' : ''}`} href="/"> 588 Home 589 </Link> 590 591 <Link 592 className={`link ${pathname === '/about' ? 'active' : ''}`} 593 href="/about" 594 > 595 About 596 </Link> 597 </nav> 598 ) 599 } 600 ``` 601 602 ```jsx filename="app/ui/nav-links.js" switcher 603 'use client' 604 605 import { usePathname } from 'next/navigation' 606 import Link from 'next/link' 607 608 export function Links() { 609 const pathname = usePathname() 610 611 return ( 612 <nav> 613 <Link className={`link ${pathname === '/' ? | `components/ticket-timeline.tsx` | 垂直时间轴，圆形指示器 + 连接线，支持 label/time/description |
